subject: Social Science (Civics)
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the concept of marginalisation and its impact on society
Examine the challenges faced by Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
Understand constitutional provisions that protect marginalised groups
Evaluate the importance of protective laws such as the SC/ST Prevention of Atrocities Act
Assess the role of government policies like reservation in reducing inequality
Develop critical thinking about social justice and equality

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Marginalisation
topics_covered: Examples of marginalised communities in India
student_activities: Students analyze examples of marginalisation in society

lesson_plan.title: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
topics_covered: Historical discrimination against SCs and STs
student_activities: Identify problems faced by SC and ST communities
Discuss why government protection is necessary

lesson_plan.title: Constitutional Protection
student_activities: List constitutional provisions that ensure equality
Discuss how these rights protect marginalised communities

lesson_plan.title: Protective Laws
topics_covered: Purpose and features of protective laws
student_activities: Explain how protective laws ensure justice

lesson_plan.title: Government Policies and Social Justice
student_activities: Debate the importance of reservation policies
Write how government schemes help reduce inequality

lesson_plan.title: Application and Chapter Review
topics_covered: Impact of laws and policies on marginalised communities
Role of citizens in promoting equality
student_activities: Write a paragraph explaining how society can reduce marginalisation

teaching_aids: Charts showing Fundamental Rights and protective laws
teacher_tip: Encourage students to examine real-life examples of inequality and discuss how laws and social policies help promote justice and equality.
